items.summary: Build a Motoko backend actor that stores and manages job requisitions including fields: title, department, location, job type (full-time/part-time/contract), description, required skills, experience level, status (draft/open/closed/on-hold), and creation timestamp. Expose CRUD operations for job requisitions.

Implement offer letter generation in the backend: accept candidate ID, job ID, salary, start date, and custom clauses, generate a structured offer letter text with all details populated, store the offer record with status (pending/accepted/declined), and expose status update operations.

Build a React frontend with a sidebar navigation layout featuring sections: Dashboard, Jobs, Candidates, Pipeline, Interviews, Analytics, Templates, Offers, and Settings. Include a top header with app logo, logged-in user role indicator, and notification bell icon.

Build a Dashboard page displaying live recruiter analytics: cards for total open jobs, total active candidates, total interviews this week, and average time-to-hire; a bar chart of candidates per pipeline stage; and a ranked list of top 5 AI-matched candidates across all open jobs.

Build a Jobs management page: display a searchable, filterable table of all job requisitions with status badges; provide a slide-over form to create or edit a job with all required fields; allow status changes inline; and include a 'View Candidates' action per job that navigates to the filtered candidate pipeline.

Build a Candidates management page: display a searchable table of candidates with AI match score, pipeline stage badge, and applied job; include a candidate detail panel showing parsed profile, skill tags, match score breakdown, timeline of stage transitions, and linked interviews; provide a resume text upload/paste area that triggers AI parsing and auto-fills the candidate form.

Build a Kanban-style Pipeline board page: display columns for each ATS stage (New, Screening, Shortlisted, Interview Scheduled, Offer Extended, Hired, Rejected); show candidate cards with name, job, and AI score; allow drag-and-drop between columns to move candidates through stages with an optional note dialog; filter board by job.

Build an Interviews page: display a list/calendar toggle of scheduled interviews showing candidate name, job, interview type, date/time, interviewer, and status; provide a form to schedule a new interview or edit existing ones; allow marking interviews as completed or cancelled.

Build an AI Chatbot widget visible site-wide as a floating button: the chatbot answers candidate FAQs about job openings, application status, and the hiring process using pre-defined intents and job data fetched from the backend; it includes a suggested-questions quick-reply panel.

Build an Analytics page with visual charts: line chart for applications over time (last 30 days), donut chart for pipeline stage distribution, bar chart for top jobs by application volume, and a table of average time-to-hire per job. Use React Query to fetch data from backend analytics endpoints.

Build a Templates management page: list all email/communication templates with category filter; provide a rich text editor form to create or edit templates with placeholder token insertion buttons; show a live preview panel rendering the template with sample data.

Build a candidate data model in the backend storing: name, email, phone, resume text, skills array, experience years, current status in pipeline (new/screening/shortlisted/interview/offer/hired/rejected), applied job IDs, AI match score per job, and timestamps. Expose CRUD and query-by-job-ID operations.

Build an Offers page: list all generated offer letters with candidate name, job, salary, start date, and status badge; provide a form to generate a new offer letter with custom clauses; display a formatted offer letter preview; allow updating offer status to accepted or declined.

Apply a bold, modern dark-mode-first design theme across the entire frontend: use a deep charcoal/slate background with vibrant teal and amber accent colors, sharp card borders, clean sans-serif typography, subtle gradient highlights on AI-scored elements, and consistent icon usage throughout all pages.

Use the generated hero logo asset (hireiq-logo.dim_200x60.png) in the sidebar header and browser tab favicon. Use the generated dashboard banner (dashboard-banner.dim_1200x300.png) as a decorative header on the Dashboard page.

Implement AI-powered resume parsing in the backend: accept raw resume text input, extract structured data (skills, experience years, education, previous roles) using keyword and pattern matching in Motoko, and return a parsed candidate profile object.

Implement AI candidate-to-job match scoring in the backend: compare a candidate's extracted skills and experience against a job's required skills and experience level, compute a percentage match score (0–100), and store the score against the candidate's application record.

Implement an applicant tracking system (ATS) pipeline in the backend: support moving a candidate through stages (New → Screening → Shortlisted → Interview Scheduled → Offer Extended → Hired / Rejected), log each stage transition with a timestamp and optional recruiter note, and expose query functions to fetch candidates by pipeline stage for a given job.

Implement interview scheduling in the backend: create interview records linked to a candidate and job with fields for date/time, interviewer name, interview type (phone/video/on-site), status (scheduled/completed/cancelled), and notes. Expose CRUD operations for interviews.

Implement recruiter analytics in the backend: expose query functions that return aggregate metrics including total open jobs, total candidates per job, candidates per pipeline stage, average time-to-hire (days from application to hired), and top-scoring candidates by match score.

Implement email/communication templates in the backend: store reusable message templates with fields for name, subject, body (with placeholders like {{candidateName}}, {{jobTitle}}), and category (application acknowledgement, interview invite, rejection, offer). Expose CRUD operations.

Implement role-based access control in the backend: define roles (Admin, Recruiter, Hiring Manager) with associated permissions. Store user-role assignments, and expose functions to check if a caller has a required permission before sensitive operations (e.g., only Admin can delete jobs, only Recruiter/Admin can move candidates).
